posts.text:
The AI job market is shifting beyond coding. Employers now demand skills in AI ethics and governance at unprecedented rates.

Our analysis of 4.4M+ job postings from 2018-2023 reveals what's driving this transformation 🧵

[LINK]

@GRAIL_center @purduepolsci

📊 The numbers tell a clear story:

• AI ethics roles: 35K (2018) → 109K (2022)
• AI governance roles: similar trajectory → 96K (2022)

Even as overall AI hiring dipped in 2023, these specialized roles remained stable.

🏦 Finance and Information sectors lead the charge

Why? Highly regulated industries can't afford ethical lapses. They need professionals who understand both technical systems AND regulatory compliance.

⚖️ AI ethics ≠ AI governance (though they're complementary)

Ethics roles focus on:
• Data privacy
• Bias mitigation
• Fairness frameworks

Governance roles emphasize:
• Risk management
• Policy development
• Regulatory compliance

🎯 Three forces driving this demand:

• Governments rolling out AI regulations
• Companies recognizing public trust as critical for AI adoption
• Universities scrambling to create relevant training programs

💡 The opportunity: 100K+ professionals needed annually in these fields

Yet many organizations struggle to find qualified talent. For those looking to upskill, this represents a significant career opportunity where demand outpaces supply.

🤔 Question for the community: Should every AI professional receive ethics/governance training, or should these remain specialized roles?

Curious what you think—does this match what you're seeing in your organizations?

[LINK]

#AIGovernance #ResponsibleAI

linkedin.text:
The AI job market is evolving beyond technical skills. Our analysis of 4.4M+ job postings from 2018-2023 shows employers increasingly demand expertise in AI ethics and governance. [LINK]

➤ AI ethics roles grew from 35K (2018) to 109K (2022)
➤ AI governance positions hit 96K in 2022
➤ Both remained stable even as overall AI hiring declined in 2023

✅ Key finding: These aren't interchangeable roles. Ethics positions emphasize data privacy, bias mitigation, and fairness frameworks. Governance roles focus on risk management, policy development, and regulatory compliance.

✅ Industry leaders: Finance and Information sectors dominate demand—highly regulated industries that can't afford ethical lapses drive this growth.

• Three forces align: Government regulations, corporate need for public trust, and educational institutions creating new programs
• The opportunity: 100K+ professionals needed annually, yet talent remains scarce

As AI adoption accelerates, ethics and governance expertise becomes non-negotiable for responsible development. Organizations seeking competitive advantage should invest in building these capabilities now.

Curious what you're seeing in your industry—does this match your hiring experience?

[Add coauthors] | GRAIL Center | Purdue University

#AIGovernance #ResponsibleAI #AIEthics #FutureOfWork #TechPolicy #AIWorkforce
